revision.text:
{{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[clans]] of Japan.
This [[family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=draft|12=draft}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from [[Saitama Prefecture]], one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through [[naturalization]] in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=draft|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# {{ill|Northern Fujiwara|ja|藤原北家|12=draft|12=draft}}: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Koretada|ja|藤原伊尹|12=draft|12=draft}}, whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=draft|12=draft}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=draft|12=draft}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=draft|12=draft}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=draft|12=draft}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=draft|12=draft}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=draft|12=draft}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=draft|12=draft}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=draft|12=draft}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=draft|12=draft}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=draft|12=draft}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=draft|12=draft}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=draft|12=draft}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=draft|12=draft}}. # {{ill|Seiwa Genji|ja|清和源氏|12=draft|12=draft}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=draft|12=draft}}.
__NOTOC__
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kadenokōji|ja|勘解由小路|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kadenokōji (position)|ja|勘解由使|12=draft|12=draft}} - An {{ill|Extraordinary official|ja|令外官|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shōjimyō Sanjō Family|ja|正親町三条家|12=draft|12=draft}} - A family once known for its long name, later changed to {{ill|Saga Family|ja|嵯峨家|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Saemon Saburō|ja|左衛門三郎|12=draft|12=draft}} - Alongside the Kadenokōji family, one of the longest surnames in Japan.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
<!--Kadenokōji family--> <!--note do not remove this category during translation--> <!--Kadenokōji family--> <!--Kadenokōji family-->

revision.text:
{{脚注の不足|date=2019年11月}} '''勘解由小路家'''（かでのこうじけ）は、日本の[[氏族]]の一つ。
漢字5文字からなるこの[[名字|苗字]]（[[家名]]）は、[[埼玉県]]に存在する[[左衛門三郎]]と並び、現代になって[[帰化]]した家系を除けば{{Refnest|たとえば元[[横綱]][[鶴竜力三郎]]が帰化した際、日本名が（[[モンゴル]]名と同じ）マンガラジャラブ・アナンダであると発表されており、「マンガラジャラブ姓の日本国民が存在する」ということになる。なお、[[中国語]]における「マンガラジャラブ」の漢字表記は漢字6文字(例:曼嘎勒扎拉布)。}}日本では最長である{{Refnest|{{Cite web|和書|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=ライブドアニュース|accessdate=2020-05-12|language=ja}}}}。姓の由来は[[平安京]]の[[勘解由小路]]である。
歴史的には、勘解由小路を号した家系には以下のものがある。
# [[藤原北家]]： ## [[世尊寺家|世尊寺流]] - [[藤原伊尹]]の子孫[[世尊寺経尹|経尹]]－[[世尊寺行尹|行尹]]と続き、行尹が勘解由小路を号したとある。 ## [[勧修寺流]] - [[藤原為房|為房]]－[[藤原為隆|為隆]]－[[藤原光房|光房]]（勘解由次官）と系図に記され、さらに子孫[[藤原高清|高清]]（勘解由小路）とある。 ## [[日野家|日野氏流]] - [[藤原資長|日野資長]]－[[藤原兼光|兼光]]－[[広橋頼資|頼資]]（勘解由小路）とあり、頼資の子[[広橋経光|経光]]－[[広橋兼仲|兼仲]]と続く[[嫡流]]は後に[[広橋家|広橋]]と号した。 ## [[日野家|日野]][[烏丸家|烏丸流]]：[[勘解由小路家 (日野流)]]を参照。 # [[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏流]]：[[勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)]]を参照。 # [[清和源氏|清和源氏流]]：[[斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛]]を参照。 __NOTOC__
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[勘解由小路]] * [[勘解由使]] - [[律令制]]下の令外官 * [[正親町三条家]] - かつてあった長い苗字。[[嵯峨家]]に改める。 * [[左衛門三郎]] - 勘解由小路家と並んで現在日本一長い名字。
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かてのこうしけ}} [[Category:日本語の姓]]

{{脚注の不足|date=2019年11月}} '''勘解由小路家'''（かでのこうじけ）は、日本の[[氏族]]の一つ。
漢字5文字からなるこの[[名字|苗字]]（[[家名]]）は、[[埼玉県]]に存在する[[左衛門三郎]]と並び、現代になって[[帰化]]した家系を除けば{{Refnest|たとえば元[[横綱]][[鶴竜力三郎]]が帰化した際、日本名が（[[モンゴル]]名と同じ）マンガラジャラブ・アナンダであると発表されており、「マンガラジャラブ姓の日本国民が存在する」ということになる。なお、[[中国語]]における「マンガラジャラブ」の漢字表記は漢字6文字(例:曼嘎勒扎拉布)。}}日本では最長である{{Refnest|{{Cite web|和書|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=ライブドアニュース|accessdate=2020-05-12|language=ja}}}}。姓の由来は[[平安京]]の[[勘解由小路]]である。

# [[藤原北家]]： ## [[世尊寺家|世尊寺流]] - [[藤原伊尹]]の子孫[[世尊寺経尹|経尹]]－[[世尊寺行尹|行尹]]と続き、行尹が勘解由小路を号したとある。 ## [[勧修寺流]] - [[藤原為房|為房]]－[[藤原為隆|為隆]]－[[藤原光房|光房]]（勘解由次官）と系図に記され、さらに子孫[[藤原高清|高清]]（勘解由小路）とある。 ## [[日野家|日野氏流]] - [[藤原資長|日野資長]]－[[藤原兼光|兼光]]－[[広橋頼資|頼資]]（勘解由小路）とあり、頼資の子[[広橋経光|経光]]－[[広橋兼仲|兼仲]]と続く[[嫡流]]は後に[[広橋家|広橋]]と号した。 ## [[日野家|日野]][[烏丸家|烏丸流]]：[[勘解由小路家 (日野流)]]を参照。 # [[賀茂朝臣氏|賀茂氏流]]：[[勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)]]を参照。 # [[清和源氏|清和源氏流]]：[[斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛]]を参照。 __NOTOC__

This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|qq=|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through [[:en:naturalization|naturalization]] in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|qq=|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|qq=|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|qq=|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|qq=|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|qq=|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|qq=|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|qq=|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|qq=|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|qq=|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|qq=|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|qq=|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|qq=|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|qq=|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through [[:en:naturalization|naturalization]] in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|qq=|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|qq=|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|qq=|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|qq=|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|qq=|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|qq=|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|qq=|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|qq=|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|qq=|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|qq=|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|qq=|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|qq=|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|qq=|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through [[:en:naturalization|naturalization]] in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through [[:en:naturalization|naturalization]] in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through [[:en:naturalization|naturalization]] in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.
__NOTOC__
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kadenokōji|ja|勘解由小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kadenokōji (position)|ja|勘解由使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji (position)|1=Kageyushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10901369}} - An {{ill|Extraordinary official|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary official|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō]] * {{ill|Shōjimyō Sanjō Family|ja|正親町三条家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjimyō Sanjō Family|1=Ōgimachisanjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545676}} - A family once known for its long name, later changed to {{ill|Saga Family|ja|嵯峨家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正親町三条家|1=Ōgimachisanjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545676}}. * {{ill|Saemon Saburō|ja|左衛門三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saemon Saburō|1=Saemon Saburō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479717}} - Alongside the Kadenokōji family, one of the longest surnames in Japan.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through [[:en:naturalization|naturalization]] in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.
__NOTOC__

# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through [[:en:naturalization|naturalization]] in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]].{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern Fujiwara]]: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From [[:en:Fujiwara no Koretada|Fujiwara no Koretada]], whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # [[:en:Seiwa Genji|Seiwa Genji]] lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11400106]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through {{ill|naturalization|en|naturalization|WD=Q841440|lt=naturalization}} in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}}.{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|WD=Q2975268|lt=Northern Fujiwara}}: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Koretada|en|Fujiwara no Koretada|WD=Q1290220|lt=Fujiwara no Koretada}}, whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.
__NOTOC__
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kadenokōji|ja|勘解由小路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kadenokōji (position)|ja|勘解由使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji (position)|1=Kageyushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10901369}} - An {{ill|Extraordinary official|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary official|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō}} * {{ill|Shōjimyō Sanjō Family|ja|正親町三条家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōjimyō Sanjō Family|1=Ōgimachisanjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545676}} - A family once known for its long name, later changed to {{ill|Saga Family|ja|嵯峨家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正親町三条家|1=Ōgimachisanjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545676}}. * {{ill|Saemon Saburō|ja|左衛門三郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saemon Saburō|1=Saemon Saburō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479717}} - Alongside the Kadenokōji family, one of the longest surnames in Japan.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|勘解由小路家|version=103660005|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:勘解由小路家]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]]
{{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture}}, one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through {{ill|naturalization|en|naturalization|WD=Q841440|lt=naturalization}} in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}}.{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|WD=Q2975268|lt=Northern Fujiwara}}: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Koretada|en|Fujiwara no Koretada|WD=Q1290220|lt=Fujiwara no Koretada}}, whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Kanshūji Lineage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Hino Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Hino Kanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Hirohashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.
__NOTOC__

{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Q11485464|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:勘解由小路家]]
{{Refnotes|date=November 2019}} '''Kadenokōji Family''' (勘解由小路家) is one of the [[:en:clans|clans]] of Japan.
This [[:en:Family name|Family name]] ({{ill|surname|ja|名字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/surname|1=myōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q69634220}}) with five kanji characters is, alongside the [[Saemon Saburō]] surname from {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|WD=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, one of the longest surnames in Japan. Excluding family names introduced through {{ill|naturalization|en|naturalization|WD=Q841440|lt=naturalization|1=Naturalization}} in modern times{{Refnest|For example, former sumo wrestler [[Hakuhō Shō]] took on the Japanese name of Mangaraja Jarub Ananda upon naturalization. While not native to Japan, this technically introduces a family name, "Mangarajajarub," into Japanese citizenship.}}It remains the longest in historical Japan{{Refnest|{{Cite web|title=日本で一番長い名字は漢字5文字のもの 一番短い名は1文字|url=https://news.livedoor.com/article/detail/8409764/|website=Livedoor News|accessdate=May 12, 2020}}}}. Its origin traces back to the [[Kadenokōji]] area in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}.{{Verification needed|Currently, one household exists in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]|date=August 3, 2023}} (originally of the {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}, a former {{ill|Viscount|ja|子爵家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Viscount|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage).
Historically, families associated with the name Kadenokōji include the following:
# {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|WD=Q2975268|lt=Northern Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}}: ## {{ill|Sessonji Family|ja|世尊寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sessonji Family|1=Sesonji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362028}} lineage: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Koretada|en|Fujiwara no Koretada|WD=Q1290220|lt=Fujiwara no Koretada|1=Fujiwara no Koretada}}, whose descendant {{ill|Keiin Sessonji|ja|世尊寺経尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiin Sessonji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was followed by {{ill|Yukitaka Sessonji|ja|世尊寺行尹|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yukitaka Sessonji|1=Yukitada Sesonji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362030}}, who adopted the name Kadenokōji. ## {{ill|Kanshūji Lineage|ja|勧修寺流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshūji Lineage|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}: From {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|ja|藤原為房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamemitsu|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}} through {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja|為隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tameyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja|光房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Mitsumoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Descendant {{ill|Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja|藤原高清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakiyo Fujiwara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is also listed as Kadenokōji in genealogies. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} lineage: From {{ill|Hino Sukemasa|ja|日野資長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Sukemasa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原資長|1=Q11623570|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623570}} through {{ill|Hino Kanemitsu|ja|藤原兼光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Kanemitsu|1=Q11623078|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}} to {{ill|Hirohashi Yoritsugu|ja|広橋頼資|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Yoritsugu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}} (noted as Kadenokōji). Yoritsugu's descendants, including {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋経光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042592}} and {{ill|Hirohashi Kanemoto|ja|広橋兼仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi Kanemoto|1=Hirohashi Kanenaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485460}}, later adopted the name {{ill|Hirohashi|ja|広橋家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohashi|1=Q11485464|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}. ## {{ill|Hino Family|ja|日野家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hino Family|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Karasumaru Lineage|ja|烏丸流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasumaru Lineage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (日野流)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Hino lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Kamo Clan|ja|賀茂朝臣氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Clan|1=Kamo no Ason clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122979457}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|ja|勘解由小路家 (賀茂氏)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadenokōji Family (Kamo lineage)|qq=}}. # {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|WD=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}} lineage: Refer to {{ill|Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|ja|斯波氏#勘解由小路武衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiba Family#Kadenokōji Bukei|1=Shiba clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1057742}}.
